title: Benstonea saint-johnii
taxonomy: Benstonea saint-johnii is a species of flowering plant in the family Pandanaceae. It is a member of the monotypic genus Benstonea, which was previously included in the genus Pandanus. The plant is native to New Guinea and has been classified as a distinct species based on its unique characteristics. Further research is needed to understand the full classification and relationships of Benstonea saint-johnii within the Pandanaceae family. The classification of Benstonea saint-johnii is an area of ongoing study and refinement in the field of botany.

intro: Benstonea saint-johnii, also known as Saint John's Benstonea, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Haloragaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in treating wounds and skin conditions such as eczema. The bioactive constituents of Benstonea saint-johnii include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, often used to treat skin conditions and wounds, as well as to reduce fever and alleviate pain.
